methodology: Platform-product OKRs with a small set of company objectives, outcome-oriented key results, and explicit team ownership across platform product areas.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe platform outcomes for internal customers, not internal feature checklists.
Key results focus on adoption, flow, controls, and reliability rather than tool rollout volume.
Every key result assigns executing and supporting teams so shared ownership stays concrete.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make the platform the easiest path for mainstream teams
companyObjectives.statement: Improve onboarding, discoverability, and paved-road quality so stream-aligned teams choose the platform because it reduces real work.

inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows that platform adoption depends on product-quality onboarding and visible reduction of setup work.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make the standard path safer and more operable by default
companyObjectives.statement: Increase platform-wide control coverage, telemetry consistency, and incident response quality without pushing teams back into manual governance queues.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Trusted supply chain baseline: validated discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Shows teams accept stronger controls when those controls are built into the default path.
Shapes the exception model required to keep controls usable.
